title: Micah Giiett
content:

{{Quote|I'll tell you the most important thing you need to know about the Jedi… we're not afraid to die for a worthy cause.|Micah Giiett's final words|<ref name="jcaow4">[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 4|''Star Wars: Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 4]]</ref>}}
'''Micah Giiett''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] before the return of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]. A highly respected member of the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], Master Giiett served for many years prior to the [[Yinchorri Uprising]] of [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]].

A master swordsman, Master Giiett had training in the ways of the [[Jedi Sentinel/Legends|Jedi Sentinel]], believing that Jedi should not rely solely on [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to get the job done. A contemporary of the [[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]], Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], and fellow Councilor [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], Giiett's final mission took him to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]]. There Giiett fought valiantly, sacrificing himself in the end so that his comrades could escape.

==Biography==
[[File:GiettKKruhk_JCAOW.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Giiett educates [[K'Kruhk]] about [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] with a lightsaber-resistant [[Vambrace/Legends|vambrace]].]]
If Micah Giiett had a traditional Jedi upbringing, he was most certainly discovered by doctors on his homeworld to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]; doctors would have notified the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] of their discovery. After being recruited by a [[Jedi recruiter|member]] of the [[Acquisition Division/Legends|Acquisition Division]], Giiett would have been inducted into the Temple's [[Jedi academy|academy]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and begun training in a [[Clan (Jedi)/Legends|clan]] of [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|younglings]]. Eventually [[Initiate Trials/Legends|graduating]] from the academy, Giiett was chosen by a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] to continue his training in the field. After years of study, Giiett passed the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]] and was promoted to a full [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knight]] of the Order.<ref name="TJP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']] outlines in detail the path to Jedi Knighthood for all Jedi of the time period Giiett joined the Order.</ref>

Following the path of the [[Jedi Sentinel/Legends|Jedi Sentinel]], Jedi Giiett eventually was promoted to the rank of Master. A well-respected man within the Order and a wielder of dual yellow-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], Giiett's unusual style of teaching made him identifiable as a Sentinel; he often would point out to his students that they could not and should not rely solely on the Force to solve their problems. One of his most prolific teaching methods involved four cups and four stones. He would tell his students that three of the stones were white, and one of them was black. Hiding each stone under one cup he would shuffle the cups and ask where the black stone was. Using the Force to track the cup they believed the black stone to be under, most students would accurately point to the cup where Master Giiett had said he put the black stone. However, through sleight of hand there was no black stone: all cups had a white stone under it. The lesson of the demonstration was that, while the Force could make things easy, a Jedi should not be so overconfident that they did not expect the unexpected or believe everything they saw. This trick derived from the Master's belief that any trained Force user could make a good gambler, but the Force didn't trump the powers of a logical and attentive mind.<ref name="jcaow1" />

[[File:SWJTDS5_P23_PS234.png|thumb|right|250px|Giiett being reported about [[Mission to Telos (53 BBY)|the mission to Telos]] by [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]].]]
In [[53 BBY/Legends|53 BBY]], Giiett and [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] summoned Jedi Masters [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Tahl]] along with Jinn's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Xanatos]] and masterless [[Orykan Tamarik]]. The two Jedi dispatched the team to [[Telos IV|Telos]] to settle a civil war on the planet. After Xanatos challenged Yoda and the briefing was concluded, Giiett soberly and reflectively asked if Xanatos was ready to become a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. Yoda replied by saying that if he was not ready then he could never become a Jedi.<ref name="jds1">[[Jedi — The Dark Side 1|''Jedi — The Dark Side'' 1]]</ref> The mission saw Xanatos turn to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and abandon the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="jds5">''[[Jedi — The Dark Side 5|''Jedi — The Dark Side'' 5]]</ref>

During his tenure on the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], Giiett would occasionally embark on missions in service to the Order. When he was away for long periods of time, the Jedi Knight [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]] would sit in for Giiett.<ref name="tnegtc">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> At one stage, Qui-Gon Jinn's Jedi Padawan [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] briefly left the Jedi to assist [[The Young]] on [[Melida/Daan/Legends|Melida/Daan]]<ref name="ja:tdotd">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Defenders of the Dead]]''</ref> in [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]].<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> When Kenobi returned, he appealed to the council to rejoin the Jedi, and Mundi sat in for Giiett at this time.<ref name="ja:tct">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Captive Temple]]</ref> A [[43 BBY/Legends|year later]], Jinn was kidnapped by the scientist [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]. Giiett had returned from a mission and resumed his duties on the council. Jinn was later rescued on [[Simpla-12]].<ref name="Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment">''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment]]''</ref>

{{Talesstart}}
At some point, Masters Giiett and Koon were assigned a mission to [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]. The famed [[Chef/Legends|chef]] [[Slabba Drewl]] had been captured by the notorious [[Corpo (Hutt)|Corpo]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], and the Jedi were petitioned to send a rescue party. Bargaining with the Hutt, the Master Giiett entered a cooking match with the Hutt's personal kitchen droid, [[MREM-02]]; if Giiett won the contest, Drewl would be freed from his cell. While Giiett prepared two small dishes of [[Squirmer Stew]], the portly Jedi instructed Master Koon to sabotage the droid's ingredients. The [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] secretly replaced the droid's [[seebo sauce]] with droid oil, and slipped [[Gundark/Legends|gundark]] droppings, [[Rakririan Burnout Sauce]] and [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] foot powder into the competition's pot. While Master Giiett's platters did not overwhelm the judges, the droid's ruined meals could not compete and the Jedi was declared victorious. Staying true to his word, Corpo released the imprisoned chef, and the Jedi returned him to Coruscant.<ref name="Jedi Chef">{{Tales|7|Jedi Chef}}</ref>
{{Talesend}}

Master Giiett was at the Temple when he selected a young woman to train as a Padawan; this [[Kuati/Legends|Kuati]] native, [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]], would be his last apprentice.<ref name="potj" />

In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] Master Giiett was assigned by the Council to a mission that would see the end of the [[Yinchorri Uprising]]. One of several Masters assigned to the mission, Master Giiett left young Swan at the Temple and boarded one of three [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|''Consular''-class cruisers]] along with Master Koon, Jedi [[Lilit Twoseas]], and her Padawan [[K'Kruhk]]. [[Mission to Yibikkoror|Heading for]] [[Yibikkoror]], Giiett demonstrated the lightsaber-resistant properties of [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Vambrace/Legends|vambraces]] to Twoseas' Padawan.<ref name="jcaow1" /> Upon their ship's arrival in orbit over Yibikkoror, Giiett's cruiser was attacked by [[Yinchorri attack ship]]s, and they were forced to take extreme measures. Baiting their attackers with the launch of an empty escape pod, the Jedi crash-landed their damaged ship on a [[Yibikkoror floating city|floating residence platform]]. The Jedi fought their way through swarms of [[Yinchorri flutter-pack|flutter-pack]]-equipped [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]]. While Master Koon used the planet's gases to blind their assailants, Master Giiett contacted Master [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia's]] cruiser for immediate rescue.<ref name="jcaow2">[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 2|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 2]]</ref>

[[File:TheDeathofMicahGiiett.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Giiett's sacrifice]]
Escaping the planet with Masters Gallia, [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]], and [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]], the Yibikkoror group headed to [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]] to aid Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu's]] team on the ground. Under the leadership of Master Koth, Masters Giiett, Koon, and Twoseas met up with Windu after fighting their way through the Yinchorri warriors. Covering their comrades' retreat, Masters Giiett and Koon joined with Master Qui-Gon Jinn and his Padawan Obi-Wan Kenobi in fighting back the savage warriors.<ref name="jcaow3">[[Jedi Council: Acts of War 3|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'' 3]]</ref> As the fighting abated for a short period, the Jedi gathered to plan strategy when a lone warrior ambushed the group and mortally wounded Giiett. Bleeding profusely, Giiett ordered his comrades to drag him next to a downed tank and leave him. Warning off Jinn's feelings of revenge Giiett handed his life-friend Plo Koon one of his sabers to be memorialized in the Temple. Holding on to the other, he sent his friends away and waited for the Yinchorri to approach. As the brutal species surrounded the tank, Giiett lured them closer with promises of revealing the secrets of his Order. As they came close Giiett told them that Jedi knew the meaning of sacrifice and ignited his lightsaber against the tank's fuel cells, destroying the tank, the Yinchorri, and himself.<ref name="jcaow4" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I recall that Master Giiett once remarked that an individual skilled in the Force could make a comfortable living as a gambler.|Kai Hudorra|<ref name="Republic 80">[[Republic 80|''Republic'' 80]]</ref>}}
With Giiett's death, all of the remaining members of the High Council sensed his [[Death/Legends|death]] and mourned him quietly. On Coruscant the mysterious [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], also felt his passing, informing his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], of his plan's success.<ref name="jcaow4" />

When the rest of the Jedi teams returned to Coruscant, Master Koon placed Giiett's surviving lightsaber on the Master's old seat in reverence to his fallen friend. [[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]] Mace Windu led the vote on who was to replace Giiett on the Council; many of the Masters suggested Master Jinn, though Windu thought Jedi Ki-Adi-Mundi would be more suitable. Mundi having served as a substitute Councilor for Giiett in the past, the Council agreed to promote Mundi to the rank of Master and seat him on the Council.<ref name="jcaow4" /> Additionally, Giiett's friend Master Koon took on the training of Padawan Swan to Knighthood.<ref name="potj" />

[[19 BBY/Legends|Many years later]], after the fall of the Republic and the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|near-extermination]] of the Jedi Order, many of the surviving Jedi went into hiding. When Master [[Dass Jennir]] encountered Master [[Kai Hudorra]] in front of the burning [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], he asked Hudorra what he was going to do with his life if not fight. Hudorra replied that Giiett had once said that a person skilled with the Force could make a successful living as a [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]].<ref name="republic80">[[Republic 80|''Republic'' 80]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Sometimes a trick is just a trick. Trust me, Padawans. You will grow in the Force. But in the meantime, don't let your infatuation with its power dull your other senses|Master Giiett lectures Padawans [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Kenobi]] and [[K'Kruhk]] on relying too strongly on the Force|<ref name="jcaow1" />}}
[[File:GiettVsTwoYinchorri-JCAOW3.png|thumb|left|300px|Giiett was proficient in wielding dual lightsabers]]
Master Giiett was a portly man, with three thick rows of hair on his otherwise shaven head. A wise Jedi Master with an unusual style of teaching, Master Giiett's methods were unorthodox but followed closely with the Jedi teachings. While a follower of the will of the Force, Giiett did not cast off all other methods to success believing that many solutions could be found through common sense or common skill, rather then through reliance on the Force's power.<ref name="jcaow1" />

A close friend with fellow Councilor Master Plo Koon, Giiett and the [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] had a friendship that bewildered many of their colleagues. Engaging in a strange parlance, the two often hurled insults at each other or appeared to one-up the other throughout their constant banter. However, the Masters claimed that this lightened the mood on the many dangerous missions they were involved in and helped them fight through the terrors they faced.<ref name="jcaow2" />

==Powers and abilities==
A master of lightsaber wielding, Master Giiett was an expert practitioner of the art of [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]], an accelerated version of the traditional [[Form VI/Legends|Form VI]] of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] which called for the use of two sabers. Building dual blades with yellow [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]] at their core, Master Giiett had chosen the path of the Sentinel, which suited his belief that the Jedi should not be solely reliant on their Force abilities.<ref name="jcaow2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Throughout several of Giiett's appearances, his name is misspelled, with the errors consisting of both "Giett" and "Giiet."

In the 44 BBY novel ''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Captive Temple]]'', Ki-Adi-Mundi is a member of the Jedi High Council. This runs counter to [[Star Wars: Jedi Council: Acts of War|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'']] in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] as Mundi is not a member of the Council and succeeds Giiett. This was later [[retcon]]ned in ''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment]]'', which confirmed Mundi sat in for Giiett when he was on missions.
